programme.title: Virgin Island

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Dave and Lynn need help with the layout and decoration of their living room, while Kay plans her first ever craft fair.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Seán and Áine plan the Salty Shamrock pub's annual St Puppies Day show. Nick struggles to light his Brits-only BBQ. Is Laura's Olympic equestrian career over before it's begun?

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, the Garners help Janice and Stephen make their sun-soaked dreams come true. On the Costa Blanca, can the Aldertons score a hole in one for golfer James?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is house hunting around the beautiful hill-top town of Cómpeta, Andalucía, with Michael and Seamus from Northern Ireland, who want a holiday home where they can entertain.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Can Jasmine Harman find Roger and Paolo's dream holiday apartment near the beach on Fuerteventura for £65,000?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps Lisa from Bedfordshire find the perfect holiday home for her and her family in northern Tenerife, known as the Island of Eternal Spring and famous for its volcanic black-sand beaches.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: At Château des Fouilloux, Sophie and Ray start work on a tower that's unfit for a princess. At Château Trois Cloches, Chris and Jolyon race to finish their long-awaited bathroom.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil look back on their best budget-stretching renovations, from a cramped three-bed semi in Long Eaton to a not-so-done-up terrace in Shropshire.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This week's competition comes from the West Midlands where first host, company director Sue, promises an evening her guests will never forget.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the West Midlands, business guru Marc intends to use his coaching skills to plan a night that will impress his guests and bag the prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the third day in the West Midlands and 48-year-old teaching assistant Kim is hoping that her summer holiday themed evening means that she will be swanning off into the sunset with the £1000 prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the fourth day in the West Midlands, and 27-year-old college manager and part-time comedian Paul is turning his home into a `Black Country boozer' as he attempts to pull in the points as well as pulling pints.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the last night in the West Midlands, and mole-catcher Michael intends to impress his guests with his night of fancy French cuisine.

programme.title: Paul Merton: Driving Amazing Trains
programme.desc: In Germany, Paul rides an unusual electric coal-transporting train and a 'car on rails' made from a Trabant, dives underground into a silver mine and drives his biggest steam train yet.

programme.title: Steam Train Diaries
programme.desc: We soak in the scenery from Goathland to Levisham, an iconic engine goes on a mission, and the lineside team are in action monitoring the UK's only venomous snake.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An 11-year-old is brought in after being struck by a car. A woman has tripped over her cat. And a doctor describes the painful decision to live away from home for the pandemic.

programme.title: Salisbury Poisonings: The Untold Story
programme.desc: Further Novichok casualties cause panic in the community. As the case becomes a murder investigation, how should the UK respond to such blatant Russian aggression.

programme.title: Do You Know This Man?
programme.desc: One woman's social media post sparks an extraordinary fight for justice in the face of horrific sexual abuse and failures by the criminal justice system.

programme.title: Love Cheats
programme.desc: On the American Pacific coast, a female fraudster and leading local pagan feigns serious illness to steal over $500,000 from her victims. And then the FBI gets involved.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Near the junction to East Midlands airport, a trailer sheds its load and causes rush hour chaos. Adam dispatches a crack team of his finest recovery ops, in the hope they will be able to resolve the situation quickly.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Dave is on the lookout for a car driving dangerously, when suddenly it pulls out in front of him. John's 100mph blue-light run panics an unrelated driver into a crash. Veteran cop Jason's `copper's instinct' leads him to suspect a motorcyclist.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Mac stops a driver for having no insurance and unearths a huge drugs haul and the chopper tracks down two notorious burglars. Elsewhere, Tim and Ange stop an alleged road-rage suspect armed with a table leg, and Phil wakes up the neighbours in his search for a couple of taxi-fare dodgers.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A pursuit through Sheffield lands two known offenders in deep trouble, Phil pulls over a Lamborghini that is not all that it seems to be, and Mac has a close shave when he comes face to face with a gang of hoodies. Elsewhere, Ange gets the runaround from a four-legged foe.

programme.title: The Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PC Andy Doran is chasing a gang of thieves fleeing a shop robbery in Ellesmere Port. And PC Chris Swash races to the scene of a head-on car collision that has left a driver trapped.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: A drone hunts a prolific criminal after their car crashes, a suspicious car with learner plates is pulled over, and PCs pursue a driver high on laughing gas.

programme.title: Police: Hour Of Duty
programme.desc: Fighting crime against the clock: the officers of Derbyshire Police Force tackle a range of incidents, from drug house stakeouts to missing children and domestic violence calls, all in one hectic real-life hour.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Mulder and Scully suspect a Midwestern teenager is responsible for a series of lightning-related deaths.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Worlds Toughest Prisons
programme.desc: Maafushi Central Prison is a high-security prison covering an area the size of ten football pitches. Surrounded by six-meter-high walls, and the sea, the facility houses up to 800 criminals under the strictest security measures.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Arid conditions promise a rough ride for Steve as he travels from Perth into the red heart of Australia. Also, Mick has a punishing climb over the Great Dividing Range.

programme.title: Trucking Hell

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Tim is almost mown down by a desperate driver, Adam's patience is tested to the limit by a drunken road-hog, and Ange gets spooked while on the lookout for a prowler, and a group of youths gets one over on the interceptors.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A gang of lorry thieves trigger a major manhunt, a young man is busted for heroin possession, a fraudster is taken into custody and a helicopter pursuit ends in an almighty accident.

programme.title: The Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PC Ian Cosgrove tries to pull over a suspected drink driver refusing to stop. Meanwhile, in Warrington, PC Chris Swash is blue-lighting to a collision involving multiple vehicles. As he makes his way to the location, another collision takes place.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: We begin with an epic battle to put the brakes on a dangerous disqualified driver who leads Northamptonshire's Interceptors on a gruelling pursuit. And a drunk driver in a three-wheeled Astra mistakes Bomber and Tony for a police escort home.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, a police dog unit is called in to track down a pair who have gone missing after a late-night crash. Meanwhile, two drunks are restrained by officers for suspected assault.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: A psychic refuses to aid Mulder and Scully in their investigation of a serial killer preying on fortunetellers.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Worlds Toughest Prisons
programme.desc: The pre-trial prison Urso Branco is one of the worst jails in all of Brazil. Riots have been taking place here almost every year since 2002. In the past 20 years, more than 100 people have died in riots behind the walls of Urso Branco.

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The Interceptors pursue a motor suspected to be involved in several car thefts, while the Operation Revive team raid a house that has been converted into a cannabis factory. Bomber tackles a suspected drink driver, who gives him the silent treatment.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Interceptors embark on a tyre-popping pursuit to catch suspects reportedly linked to an attempted stabbing. Dog Handler Lucy and her canine crew pursue a driver legging it on foot.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: It's foot to the floor time as Interceptors Richie and Harrison join the pursuit of a Toyota that fails to stop. The cops are horrified to discover a 14-year-old at the wheel.

programme.desc: Secret filming exposes a £300,000 claim when a man is caught doing everything he says he can't, while a pet owner refuses to believe her beloved animal isn't insured. (S2 Ep12)

programme.title: Fare Dodgers: At War With The Law
programme.desc: Investigators seek a fare dodger thought to be scamming Transport for London with over £5,000 in unpaid fares. And a man caught without a valid ticket proves uncooperative.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Operation Fuego's first hit involves John and South Yorkshire officers givingchase to a getaway driver in a stolen vehicle taken in a burglary, which is now on a 120mph rampagealong the M18 near Doncaster.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Car theft is a major problem in Derbyshire and the police are investing considerable resources to tackle the problem. A number of thieves in the city know how to fool the police.

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Four joyriders have a lucky escape from the wreckage of a Ford Mondeo, stolen property is found in the back of a vehicle and the interceptors have to rush to stop a suicidal man from jumping on to the M25.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Police race to intercept three men suspected of robbing a string of plumbers' merchants. A fleeing van speeds the wrong way round a major roundabout and causes a huge crash. Police investigate illegally customised motors in Basildon.

programme.title: Rogue Claimers

programme.desc: John offers to support Mali through his breakup and Mali reassures him that he doesn't need it - it was a mutual decision between him and Abigail.

programme.title: Home and Away

programme.title: 999: Emergency Call Out

programme.desc: Barry is rushed into resus with suspected sepsis. Meanwhile, Lucy suffers significant nerve damage from a horse-riding accident that could leave her paralysed for the rest of her life.

programme.title: Skin A&E

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.desc: Ben travels to a remote island in Australia's Great Barrier Reef to live with former Sydney businessman David Glasheen. Since losing a fortune in the 1987 stock market crash, which also cost him his marriage, David has led a Robinson Crusoe-like existence. Ben learns how to pick oysters, brew beer, trade fish and dance with the locals, finding out how it feels to truly live alone.

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben travels north to Alaska to live with Bretwood Higman, Erin McCittrick and their two children. Bretwood and Erin hiked 4,000 miles to get here, and the family now lives for half the year in a Mongolian-style yurt on the mountainside, with the other half spent hiking in the wilderness. While staying with them, Ben learns how to chop trees and protect himself from grizzly bears.

programme.desc: Lawyer, wife and mother Faith Howells is drawn into a mystery when her husband and business partner Evan disappears. He leaves for work but never arrives. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler attempt to question a confused young woman about an attack on her parents that left her brother traumatised and Stabler puts his career on the line when he goes too far.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson is called in after a young woman shows up at the hospital, raped and beaten with her head shaved. When Benson returns to her apartment, she encounters Star Morrison who admits that Haley is a federal informant working with them on eco-terrorism.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.desc: Timmy sees a particularly grumpy farmer giving one of the flock a hard time and decides to give him an idea of what it's like to be a sheep. However, things aren't quite so simple when the time comes to have the old farmer back.

programme.title: Uaine
programme.desc: Calum visits the Findhorn Community in Morayshire. It was started by just three people in 1962, who wanted a greener lifestyle and community and today, there are more than 400 people living there.

programme.title: Bogaisean is Gumbalan
programme.desc: Jolli is sick of her achievements being shared with Merri, so she sets out to prove herself as a solo gumbler. When she ditches her brother, he is captured by the snikes, and on her rescue mission she realises it is better when they work together.

programme.title: Miann na Maighdinn-mara
programme.desc: Kate Macleod, The Hebridean Mermaid, takes us on a journey to some of the hidden gems of the Hebrides.

programme.title: SpeakGaelic
programme.desc: Joy Dunlop continues to guide viewers through further steps to speak Gaelic.

programme.title: An La
programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Port Steòrnabhaigh
programme.desc: Featuring one of the largest construction projects the Western Isles have ever seen, plus a delve into the fascinating world of archaeology.

programme.title: Kathleen
programme.desc: A'deanamh luaidh air an t-seinneadair Ghàidhlig chliùiteach Kathleen NicAonghais à Uibhist a Deas. A celebration of acclaimed Gaelic singer Kathleen MacInnes.

programme.title: Dàna: Scotland's Wild Side
programme.desc: Seeking grand adventure, Coinneach Rankin travels to Norway to make an ascent of the country's national mountain, Stetind.

programme.title: Ciad-fathan nan Creutairean
programme.desc: Rannsachadh air na dòighean annasach a tha fradharc bheathaichean air leasachadh. An exploration of the strange evolution of animal vision.

programme.title: Brunch le Abigail
programme.desc: Tha Abigail a' toirt thugainn beachd annasach airson treat, Dutch Baby. Abigail shares an idea for an unusual treat, a Dutch baby.

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis and Muireann NicAmhlaoibh are accompanied by musician Michelle Mulcahy and fiddler Dónal O'Connor as they visit Skye and Lochaber. In Skye, they meet Anne Martin and Angus Nicolson, while in Lochaber they meet fiddlers Angus Grant and Ewan Henderson for conversation and some lively musical sessions.

programme.title: Seo BBC Alba
programme.desc: A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: Seo BBC Alba
programme.desc: A new view from Scotland.

programme.desc: BBC FOUR is the BBC channel for people who want more. More depth, more range, more to stimulate the mind.

programme.title: This Is BBC Four
programme.desc: BBC FOUR is the BBC channel for people who want more. More depth, more range, more to stimulate the mind.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jesus College, Cambridge and St Catherine's College, Oxford are both hoping to earn themselves a place in the second round. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: The Crossworders face another wordy bunch in the form of the Edinburgh Scrabblers.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth finds herself with an ideal opportunity to impress Emmet with her singing when she discovers that he is involved in a concert at the church hall. However, Richard has other things on his mind, dreading the thought of early retirement and having to spend more time with his wife.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy acts as a marriage counsellor to his parents, and a marital aide to his friends.

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Another easy-to-digest slice of the past from erudite scholar Simon Schama. He traces the steps of the empiremakers, asking how a trading enterprise based on the idea of liberty became an empire built on the enslavement of millions of Africans. He also explores how Britain lost control of what became the United States, yet went on to conquer large swathes of the Indian subcontinent.

programme.title: The Corinthians: We Were the Champions
programme.desc: The real-life story of the original rebel girls of football, Corinthians Ladies FC, who defied the English Football Association's 50-year ban on women playing to become global champions.

programme.title: Berlusconi: Condemned to Win
programme.desc: Silvio Berlusconi dodges corruption charges, wins again with Milan and blurs the line between football and politics as controversy and his cult grow.

programme.title: Gluck: Who Did She Think He Was?
programme.desc: The untold story of Britain's cross-dressing high society painter. Gluck was one of the British Establishment's go-to portrait painters of the 1930s. Her shows were attended by royalty, aristocrats and celebrities.

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Bordering the Caspian Sea, Baku offers a tantalising mix of the ancient and modern - at the crossroads of east meets west, on the ancient silk trading route. It is also an authoritarian state, where cultural life is tightly controlled.

programme.title: India: Nature's Wonderland
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin, Freida Pinto and Jon Gupta reveal the hidden wonders of India's surprising natural world. This is a land where the tea comes with added elephants, gibbons sing to greet the morning, tigers dance and lions roam.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: My Timeshare is Worth Less Than When I Bought it
programme.desc: The team investigate the holiday timeshare company that members feel has moved the goalposts as they reached the end of their contracts - with their investments worth far less than they had expected.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Is a house in Wales the smallest Martin has ever encountered in his 20 years on the show? Meanwhile, Dion is captivated by a rather funky, retro carpet in a terrace in Lancashire. And Tommy's in Wandsworth, London, where a one-bedroom garden flat has potential to add space.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and the teams head to an antiques fair in Builth Wells in Mid Wales. Experts Ben Cooper and Kate Bliss are on hand to help the reds and blues pick the best bargains to take to auction, and Natasha pays a visit to a special museum in Kidderminster to learn about the history of carpet-making in the Worcestershire town.

programme.desc: The annual Royal Horticultural Society Chelsea Flower Show is considered the most famous flower show in the United Kingdom, attracting visitors from all over the world.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: The presenters take the prospective buyers around to different houses in the country. The desire for more room, cleaner air and less noise drives city dwellers to seek an escape to the country.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.sub-title: North West - The Biggest Clock Steve Has Ever Worked On
programme.desc: Will Manchester Town Hall's giant clock prove too big for Steve to handle? Will hears the tragic story behind a fruit machine and discovers the endangered craft of globe-making.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Family of four Melissa, Matt, Becky and Nick take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they must cross the Bridge, stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Alan and the final five designers head to Leeds to transform studio rental flats. They must cleverly divide open-plan spaces and inject character into show flats to entice potential renters. Former Interior Design Masters winner Lynsey Ford joins Michelle to critique the designs, and they are blown away by the dramatically different results.

programme.title: The Krays
programme.desc: The disturbing story of the making of the infamous 60s underworld figures Reggie and Ronnie Kray - from their upbringing by a domineering mother in the deprivation of London's East End. The gangland empire they build is characterised by violence and intimidation, and the film is unflinching in its depiction of their rise to power.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: I Cancelled My Card, but Scammers Kept Spending
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate the mysterious transactions made via mobile phone payments that happened after the customer had cancelled their bank card. Experts explain what can be done to protect customers. Also on the programme, the small business owners being blackmailed on social media with fake reviews, the fraudulent websites taking clean air zone payments, and the new technology designed to help us spot scams.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: We head to the Kent coast to revisit a renovation that's taken some time to finish. Jacqui checks out a large terrace as well as a tiny flat in a tale of two contrasting projects.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles remembers the historic day in 1972 when Llanelli defeated the mighty All Blacks at Stradey Park in one of rugby's famous upsets. He meets Roy Bergiers, the Scarlet who scored the game-winning try, and sees Roy's collection of match memorabilia that he's kept all these years. Meanwhile, the red and blue teams vie for victory at an antiques fair in Llandeilo. Expert Kate Bliss is joined by dad and daughter Rob and Charlotte, while Richard Madley captains couple Neil and Pru.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Is a house in Wales the smallest Martin has ever encountered in his 20 years on the show? Meanwhile, Dion is captivated by a rather funky, retro carpet in a terrace in Lancashire. And Tommy's in Wandsworth, London, where a one-bedroom garden flat has potential to add space.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and the teams head to an antiques fair in Builth Wells in Mid Wales. Experts Ben Cooper and Kate Bliss are on hand to help the reds and blues pick the best bargains to take to auction, and Natasha pays a visit to a special museum in Kidderminster to learn about the history of carpet-making in the Worcestershire town.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Alan and the final five designers head to Leeds to transform studio rental flats. programme.title: The Krays
programme.desc: The disturbing story of the making of the infamous 60s underworld figures Reggie and Ronnie Kray - from their upbringing by a domineering mother in the deprivation of London's East End. programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: I Cancelled My Card, but Scammers Kept Spending
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate the mysterious transactions made via mobile phone payments that happened after the customer had cancelled their bank card. Experts explain what can be done to protect customers. Also on the programme, the small business owners being blackmailed on social media with fake reviews, the fraudulent websites taking clean air zone payments, and the new technology designed to help us spot scams.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: We head to the Kent coast to revisit a renovation that's taken some time to finish. Jacqui checks out a large terrace as well as a tiny flat in a tale of two contrasting projects.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles remembers the historic day in 1972 when Llanelli defeated the mighty All Blacks at Stradey Park in one of rugby's famous upsets. He meets Roy Bergiers, the Scarlet who scored the game-winning try, and sees Roy's collection of match memorabilia that he's kept all these years. Meanwhile, the red and blue teams vie for victory at an antiques fair in Llandeilo. Expert Kate Bliss is joined by dad and daughter Rob and Charlotte, while Richard Madley captains couple Neil and Pru.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: I Cancelled My Card, but Scammers Kept Spending
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate the mysterious transactions made via mobile phone payments that happened after the customer had cancelled their bank card. Experts explain what can be done to protect customers. Also on the programme, the small business owners being blackmailed on social media with fake reviews, the fraudulent websites taking clean air zone payments, and the new technology designed to help us spot scams.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: We head to the Kent coast to revisit a renovation that's taken some time to finish. Jacqui checks out a large terrace as well as a tiny flat in a tale of two contrasting projects.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles remembers the historic day in 1972 when Llanelli defeated the mighty All Blacks at Stradey Park in one of rugby's famous upsets. He meets Roy Bergiers, the Scarlet who scored the game-winning try, and sees Roy's collection of match memorabilia that he's kept all these years. Meanwhile, the red and blue teams vie for victory at an antiques fair in Llandeilo. Expert Kate Bliss is joined by dad and daughter Rob and Charlotte, while Richard Madley captains couple Neil and Pru.

programme.desc: Interior designer Banjo and husband Ro arrive on Ulva to revive a derelict mansion, battling a tiny budget, island logistics and their first big test - the crumbling library.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Fufe, it's a critical year for Andy and Jo, who have taken out a £2 million loan to get into arable farming. In Dumfries and Galloway, Nick and Holly are preparing to take over the family farm and experimenting with a new breed of sheep.

programme.title: Janey
programme.desc: Janey Godley is one of Britain's leading comedians. This film follows Janey on her 2023 Not Dead Yet tour, following her diagnosis of terminal cancer. Janey is touring with her best mate Shirley and her daughter, comedian Ashley Storrie.

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.sub-title: The Secret Lives of Lighthouse Keepers
programme.desc: A glimpse into the working world of the lighthouse keepers, technicians, seafarers, and designers who are responsible for keeping the lights on in 208 lighthouses round the coast of Scotland and the Isle of Man.

programme.desc: Conversation about Scotland's biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: T in the Park

programme.title: TUNE
programme.desc: Dundee star Hannah Laing's career has taken her across the globe. As she prepares to headline a historic homecoming show at the Caird Hall, she reveals how she switched from dental nurse to disc jockey.

programme.desc: It's the grand final! Interior designer Anna Campbell Jones, architect Danny Campbell and Isle of Mull-based interior designer Banjo Beale have scoured the country in search of Scotland's home of the year. It's now down to the final six homes - the winners of each regional episode - to go head-to-head, but only one of them can take the prestigious title.

programme.desc: Former wildlife assistant April has been newly promoted to the head of the large mammals section and, with the other team members, she anxiously awaits the influx of orphans that accompanies the arrival of March each year.

programme.title: Showpeople: Licensed to Thrill
programme.desc: It's Danger Night in Bathgate, and Christian Horne is struggling to open after two days of torrential rain.

programme.title: General Assembly
programme.desc: Coverage of the General Assembly of the Church of Scotland, including films on the legacy of slavery and supporting asylum seekers.

programme.title: Scotcast
programme.desc: Conversation about Scotland's biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: Officers deal with an endless Christmas, a drug dealer's infinite excuses and the limitless nonsense of local nuisance Bobby Muir.

programme.title: Deadly 60

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to a place very few people have ever been to: Snake Island.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: In the penultimate leg, the race shifts up a gear as the four remaining teams enter their eighth and final country, Mongolia. Facing freezing temperatures and rugged off-road travel, the intrepid duos set out on an expedition across the endless steppe and deep into the unknown, traversing landscapes few travellers have ever experienced. It tests every ounce of their resilience as they battle to take pole position at the final checkpoint before the finish line.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip Fitz-Amobi, a brilliant woman, is unsure whether schoolgirl Andie Bell was killed by her lover Sal Singh five years ago. They must see how far they will go to protect Pip from learning the truth if Sal Singh turns out not to be the murderer.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip Fitz-Amobi, a brilliant woman, is unsure whether schoolgirl Andie Bell was killed by her lover Sal Singh five years ago. They must see how far they will go to protect Pip from learning the truth if Sal Singh turns out not to be the murderer.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip Fitz-Amobi, a brilliant woman, is unsure whether schoolgirl Andie Bell was killed by her lover Sal Singh five years ago. They must see how far they will go to protect Pip from learning the truth if Sal Singh turns out not to be the murderer.

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Eight months before the UK's worst riots in over a decade, award-winning journalist Mobeen Azhar heads to Kirkby, 28 miles east of Liverpool city centre, to investigate why a peaceful protest outside an asylum seeker hotel turned into a riot.

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar investigates how online rumours and disinformation combined with extremist agitation led to real-world consequences for the people of Kirkby.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. She goes to a high-octane pool party to discover how casinos are cashing in on the party scene.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Tir goes even further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting those who live and work there.

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: How a Somerset high-flyer turned his charm and connections into tools of deception - conning his school friends and hundreds of others while living a life of glamour himself.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: The presenters take the prospective buyers around to different houses in the country. The desire for more room, cleaner air and less noise drives city dwellers to seek an escape to the country.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine is at the helm as the red and blue teams shop for antiques in Herefordshire. It all comes down to a battle of the bonus buys at the auction in Wrexham.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a stunning railway adventure through the western Mediterranean islands of Sardinia and Corsica, beginning in Sardinia's capital, Cagliari.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: One Clue, One Cow and a Soil-Based Breakthrough
programme.desc: Farm thieves strike and a cow's in deep water - police and fire crews race to the rescue.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The detectorists battle the elements to find a long-lost ring on the fells, thrown from the hand of a shepherd while tending his sheep 60 years ago. Social media detective Mui takes on a phone found by an underwater drone, which leads her on her first international search for an owner. Meanwhile, wood crafter Stef showcases some of the finest traditional lake district crafts from our discarded finds.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Engaging conversation about the political issues that matter to you.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie hosts as the series continues with another four contenders taking to the black chair. The specialist subjects are Neil Armstrong, Shakespeare's comedies, the Jacobite Rebellion of 1745 and Rupert Bear.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: Single mum Hayley and her daughter Rosie Bell live in a former council house with virtually no storage space. The team help them to reclaim some empty wall space and find a way to upcycle a piece of furniture that gives them a place to eat together.

programme.title: Wonders of Life
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox travels across the US and encounters some astonishing creatures that reveal how the senses evolved. It is a story that takes him through life's journey, from single-celled organisms to sentient beings.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel examines a finger exerciser, a costume worn by Yul Brynner, a poacher's gun disguised as a walking stick, silver teething rattles, a valuable pottery cat, a rare Anglo-Saxon ring and a collection of Oxo memorabilia.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and his team of experts invite members of the public to bring their antiques to be viewed and valued, with an option to sell at auction.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Josh Jones, Molly Rainford, Mark Rhodes and Sonia take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway tour of Sardinia heads inland towards the island's dramatic mountain landscapes, which he discovers were frequented by DH Lawrence.

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus loves a British fruit-based pudding and is searching Provence for the local equivalent. After trying out a prawn cocktail served in a half-melon on local farmers, he changes tack and meets with a local chef for a France v Britain pudding cook-off. He's also looking for the tastiest local cheese to take to the village pétanque game. He discovers a Provençal favourite is goat's cheese, and so he visits a local producer who gives him a whirlwind apprenticeship in all things goat.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: The annual Royal Horticultural Society Chelsea Flower Show is considered the most famous flower show in the United Kingdom, attracting visitors from all over the world.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig is in search of victory with Rob Beckett, Daliso Chaponda, Lara Ricote and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist visits uncharted territory in pursuit of new discoveries. Steve Backshall takes on physical challenges, encounters extraordinary wildlife and meets remarkable people.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the day's top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: The world's most famous football show, with the big names and the big games from the Premier League.

programme.title: Later ... with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools Holland brings together hot, legendary and undiscovered artists for live studio performances, interviews and musical magic.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: In the penultimate leg, the race shifts up a gear as the four remaining teams enter their eighth and final country, Mongolia. Facing freezing temperatures and rugged off-road travel, the intrepid duos set out on an expedition across the endless steppe and deep into the unknown, traversing landscapes few travellers have ever experienced. It tests every ounce of their resilience as they battle to take pole position at the final checkpoint before the finish line.

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the Tywi river valley, from Llansteffan's coastal dunes to fallow deer ruts, ancient oak woodlands, and the remote heights of the Tywi Forest.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: Meticulous and methodical, Big John engineers his ransom scheme with military precision. On paper, it's flawless. But as the deadline approaches, nothing goes according to plan, and two battles unfold simultaneously: a high-stakes ransom exchange and a desperate race to stop a bomb designed to be unstoppable. Precision planning meets a chaotic reality, and one miscalculation on either side could mean catastrophe.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons face a difficult dilemma in the middle of a life-saving bile duct cancer operation. A chest sarcoma proves more challenging than expected, with no room for error.

programme.desc: Bing and Pando experiment with Bing's toy train on the Fun Fort in the playground. The train is good at going over bridges and brilliant at going down the slide. But when Bing slides it off the roof of the playhouse, the train crashes and breaks. Bing is upset until he realises that the pieces fit together to make something completely different.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky keeps standing in the way when Laa-Laa is trying to look at the flowers, but Laa-Laa likes looking at Tinky Winky just as much as she likes looking at flowers! The Teletubbies watch a Tummy Tale in which children play hide and seek.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee wakes up in a grumpy mood. He's even too grumpy for breakfast! It's up to Mo to put a smile on Tee's face, but this proves more difficult than she thought. Every time she gets him close to smiling, he slips back into the grumps again. How is Mo going to cheer Tee up and get him to eat breakfast?

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea gets a visit from her parents, and they team up to beat Supertato.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Sula and Bing are camping in Bing's garden in order to see the stars and the moon. As Flop goes back for Hoppity, night-time noises seep from the dark garden, spooking Bing. Then, a scary shape on the tent terrifies him. They go inside, but the monster has followed them in! Bing and Sula discover that Flop is the monster. So they go back outside, where the billion, zillion stars and a huge moon are waiting for them.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Bryan the ferry can't shut his engine off, so he's speeding out of control around the lake. How will Mojo and Bo get aboard to take control and steer Bryan to safety?

programme.title: Hey Duggee's Squirrel Club
programme.desc: Introducing you to Squirrel Club are Happy's Mum and Norrie's Dad. There is a lot to see, starting with Duggee walking along, but he's in two halves. Next is an instalment of the Squirrels' favourite comic book, Detective Puppy.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are at the Clubhouse playing, but they can't stop thinking about the dreams they had last night. Luckily, Duggee has his dream badge and can help the Squirrels work out what they mean. As the Squirrels think about dreams, all the different dreams of the Clubhouse friends become weirder and more wonderful. We even get to see Duggee's dream.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo love to video chat with Muffin and Socks so they can all draw pictures together. When Muffin won't give Socks a turn, the kids discover what happens when they hog.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo pretend that they are living on a desert island in the back garden and they discover Dad washed up on the beach. Dad needs to embrace his inner kid if he wants to make Bingo and Bluey's Rug Island his home, too.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.sub-title: The Leaning Tower of Pisa, Italy
programme.desc: The Go Jetters race to fix the Leaning Tower of Pisa. But has it really been glitched?

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Peter is asked to watch Jemima's egg, he discovers that babysitting is far more dangerous than he could ever have imagined.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: When the Octonauts are unable to rescue a group of narwhals trapped behind arctic ice, they recruit a bowhead whale to help them break through.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Seven shows the others how to be lucky: ask a number friend to jump on your head! But how do you know which friend? Find out how to tell the difference between two numbers with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: OO and friends discover a talking book and some missing words and a pile of letters. Can they put the story of Little Red Riding Hood back together?

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.desc: Katerina Flamingo sings a song about routines in Here We Go Round the Mulberry Bush.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella and Lift are visited by a shining star and set off to explore the floors of Playtime Towers. The first stop is Sketch in Boxtropolis - what will she be making today?

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Sula, Pando, Flop and Padget all have a knack, a special thing that only they can do. Bing is frustrated when all his own knacks are easy for everyone else to do too. When Pando accidentally hurts Bing's ear they are all in for a surprise, as Bing discovers he does have a knack that no-one else can do.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: A celebration of communicating and encouraging children to talk with a focus on one word, showing various visualisations of a word in a rhythmical, bouncy, humorous way!

programme.title: Lu & the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Lu has fun playing with Elodie's cousin, leaving Elodie feeling left out.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Lou's drumming makes Kiri feel funny and wobbly all over. Pania explains that there's nothing wrong, it's called dancing.

programme.title: Froglets
programme.desc: Ziff, Pipp and Button become painters with honey, colourful petals and an old pizza box.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: Stop-motion preschool programme about the adventures of four inquisitive Twirlywoos. The Twirlywoos meet a soft and fluffy cat. Back in the Boat the Box brings them a soft surprise.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin, George and friends explore different emotions, while Mr Tumble tries to stay calm.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: During the Tiddlytubbies' Sleepybyes, RuRu is wide awake. Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po take it in turns to sing him a lullaby. When the Teletubbies sing the lullaby together, it sends RuRu off to sleep.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Marta, Billie, Hud, Elijah, Thomas, Finley and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna's bag is a rubber duck, and all the parents and babies have different bath toys in their bags which are explored through discovery, play, song and story.

programme.title: Tiny Tunes
programme.desc: Lisa and the children float rubber ducks and sign and sing `Five Little Ducks'.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: It's Novah and Nelly's turn to accompany Aaron on a unique garden adventure. Today, the team will need to be all ears as they take in the sounds their city centre plot has to share.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Rebecca has lost her healthy, fruity snacks. Murphi can't find them either, but he does find a huge portrait of his great grandma with the big eyes. This gives him an eye-dea for a make - a pair of silly eyeglasses! The Mini Monsters make gorgeous-looking muffins - but not from the kind of sponge you'd expect. Rebecca gets a call on the Magic Monster Mirror from her aunt, who has lost her ducks - she decides to replace them with a quiet, peaceful model version instead.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: At school, Bluey and friends throw themselves into various games. Teacher Calypso keeps them on track with a wise word and a song, all except the mysterious Pretzel.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Chipmunk is cycling one day delivering his packages when he hits a rock and his trike falls apart! He has so many packages to deliver - what is he going to do now?

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is a big, friendly dog who runs The Squirrel Club, an after-school activity club where children can learn and take part in all kinds of activities, have fun adventures, and earn badges for their achievements.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Audrey has a table tennis lesson with her coaches Tim and Harry. First, she practices some bat and ball skills before enjoying a rally against Harry, who is an actual table tennis champion. Later, the whole family get sporty and have some fun playing mini-golf, and Audrey puts Mum through her paces with a very bendy yoga lesson.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Choekyi has been shown a traditional way to make momos, which are a type of dumpling. First, Choekyi makes dough that is then rolled out into circles. She fills these circles with a mixture of spinach, cheese and mushrooms. When they tried mushrooms at first, Choekyi's friends did not really like the taste or smell. Let's hope they prefer them when they are cooked in the momos. Choekyi's recipe comes from Tibet, so when her friends arrive she greets them in a traditional Tibetan way.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie is finding out about the life cycle of plants! She visits a gigantic tomato growing farm and uses her special time-lapse camera to discover how a plant germinates from a seed and grows into a shoot. Next, she travels in a lift to the top of the high greenhouse to see the flowers and tomato fruits growing. We learn how the plant needs water, sunlight and carbon dioxide to make its own energy to grow.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Therapy dog Toffee and his owner Helen help Betty and Pop-Pop feel brave enough to perform at their talent show at the Woofton Care Home.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Spider Monkey and Tamandua
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet a troop of spider monkeys and watch how they use their long tails like a fifth hand to grab and balance as they swing through the trees. Next, Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet Gilly the tamandua. They see how this tree-living ant eater finds food using his long, sticky tongue. Finally, Aneeshwar and Lizzie discover what the wild connection is between these two incredible animals.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's the morning of Evie's first day at school and the Brooklyn-Bells are on schedule. But Waffle isn't happy. He's expecting his usual morning walkies. Doug tells Waffle that Gramps will be walking him today, he'll be coming to the house later. Waffle is insistent, he wants to walk to school with the rest of the family.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona is practicing her diving skills, and she has a supportive audience watching. But when Oona struggles to dive deep enough to reach Baba's pebble, she gets frustrated.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: Chip sings a heartfelt song about being a potato. Though he can't do many physical things, he is proud of who he is.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Yukee's garden friends help turn her 'a day in my life' homework assignment into an exciting, musical sound adventure.

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: While out hillwalking with Mum, Maddie and Triggs meet a new friend who teaches them all about yodeling, an ancient method of communicating over long distances.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Next, the Squirrels watch the bugs on the tree and put on a play to catch the differences. The Squirrels watch the play, but when they watch it again, the bugs are different. They are now a banana and a clown. The Squirrels see it right away as they are getting better. Now it's time to play ultimate spot the difference. The Squirrels go outside the Clubhouse with Duggee and watch all the animals take formation. We see a complete match on the right and left sides.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Five and friends set off on a rhyming romp through field and forest but they keep getting mixed up.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The adventures of 26 lively letters who can make words come to life by holding hands. From A to Z, every Alphablock has fun discovering the magic of wordplay.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends visit a planetarium where they learn all about outer space. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble tries to spot a shooting star using his new telescope.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po are outside in the cold when they spot the Tiddlytubbies who are inside and warm. The Teletubbies put on hats, gloves and scarves to keep warm.

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how mosses play an important role in the environment. They find out that they can be home to lots of different creatures and that birds can even use moss to build their nests.

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Elvis refuses to play football after his lucky stinky socks get washed. Evie B leads the hunt for the smelliest smell in the Den so they can make the socks stinky again.

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace shows off three amazing machines that are all made to race. Grace has a race on her lake on a stand-up jet ski, shows off the incredibly fast Bloodhound car and goes for a ride on a sidecar motorbike.

programme.title: Dodge's Pup School
programme.desc: There's wet paint on the new climbing frame - and Dodge accidentally knocks into it!

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster comes back from holiday to find chocolates waiting for him, and because it's `Sharing With Others Day' he should share them with his friends, but they look very tempting.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: While tracking in the Congolese jungle, the Octonauts come upon a mysterious creature.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of Nutkin's Rabbity Day
programme.desc: When the Squirrel Tribe forget his birthday, Nutkin quits the squirrel life and decides to become a rabbit.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: When the Squirrels can't figure out if fish have noses, Duggee shows them his library. But when the Squirrels realise no-one is visiting it, they have an idea. A mobile library. The Squirrels set off with Duggee. Along the way, they help Tino find his creativity, King Tiger figure out his favourite ice cream flavour and Worm find her love of fantasy. The Squirrels learn just how helpful and informative a library can be.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: When Zigzag's toes are sore, Vida gets her back on track for the Sweetwood relay race.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Banana Superstar's show is stopped when Evil Pea steals his peel. Supertato races against him to get it back, but trouble hits when a giant banana statue collapses onto the stage.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo and Bo join the big cross-town race with plans to win. But how can they stay ahead when they have to keep stopping and swopping to help other racers in trouble?

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo love to video chat with Muffin and Socks so they can all draw pictures together. When Muffin won't give Socks a turn, the kids discover what happens when they hog.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo pretend that they are living on a desert island in the back garden and they discover Dad washed up on the beach. Dad needs to embrace his inner kid if he wants to make Bingo and Bluey's Rug Island his home, too.

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: With Pearl entranced by Fifi's glowing tummy mushrooms, Zog fears the worst as Gadabout attempts to mend Boom's wing himself.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: When Dr Ollie discovers the Story Bus is delayed, he vows to save the day by inventing a new story to tell Miss Hoolie and the children.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: When Jess takes Evie for her first school visit, Waffle goes along too, on the condition that he doesn't sing. He tries his best to behave, but it's hard for a Wonder Dog! On their walk to school, Jess and Evie bump into Connie Lester and her daughter Ruby. Ruby reveals that she will also be starting at Sommersby School too, in the year above Evie. Ruby enjoys stroking Waffle and tells Evie that she loves animals. She has a pet dog of her own, Jelly, a yellow labrador.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi swaps places with Aunty T and learns that being yourself is the best way to have fun.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: Gran Gran busts a groove when she teaches JoJo the moves to a traditional Saint Lucian dance. JoJo finds that learning a dance can take time, until she realises she can put her own twist on it.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: When Sarah has a cold, Duck helps her enjoy a visit to the doctor.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small is out fishing and Baby Soup Dragon is helping his Mum, so Tiny has no one to play with. He plays catch by himself but it's not much fun. Major Clanger notices that Tiny looks bored and makes him a special cone-shaped gadget which launches a cotton ball up into the air for the user to catch in the cone. Mother calls Tiny to ask for some help in the garden and Tiny leaves the gadget on the floor while she goes off to help her Mum. But when she gets back, the gadget has disappeared.

programme.title: Beddybyes

programme.desc: Gia struggles to choose between two men and travels along a path that will lead to true love.

programme.title: Cinebuzz
programme.desc: The host interviews various media personalities, shares the latest news of the Bollywood industry and reviews the newest movies.

programme.title: Marzi The Power
programme.desc: Krishnaveni runs away from home to elope with her lover, Kishore, who does not show up. Gani, a self-centred man, plans to take her to her father, hoping to make a quick buck.

programme.title: Chhota Bheem and the Curse of Damyaan
programme.desc: A brave and strong young boy and his friends face off against an evil sorcerer.

programme.title: Rishtey
programme.desc: In spite of their class differences, Suraj and Komal get married, but Komal's father is constantly trying to break up their marriage.

programme.title: Jab Pyaar Kisise Hota Hai
programme.desc: A Playboy's past catches up with him when he wants to get married.

programme.title: Luka Chuppi
programme.desc: A television reporter in Mathura falls in love with a headstrong woman.

programme.title: Goliyon Ki Raasleela Ram-Leela
programme.desc: Romantic Drama inspired by William Shakespeare's Romeo and Juliet. A young couple's happiness is thwarted by their warring families.

programme.title: Ishq Vishk
programme.desc: It is about Rajiv and Payal who are friends since childhood. While Payal is in love with Rajiv, it takes him a bit longer to return her feelings.

programme.title: Warning
programme.desc: Friends become stranded in dangerous waters with no means to get back aboard their yacht.

programme.title: Izzatdaar
programme.desc: Brahm Dutt, a rich steel magnate, is falsely convicted for the murder of his best friend. In prison, he finds out that his son-in-law Indrajeet has conspired against him and vows to avenge himself.

programme.title: Krishna Ka Badla
programme.desc: A young man stages a play in Bellary with his drama troupe in order to fulfil his late grandfather's dying wish. However, his life changes when he crosses paths with an investigative journalist.

programme.title: Lucknow Central
programme.desc: An inmate in a Lucknow jail yearns to be set free. When he learns that a few prisoners must form a band, he sees it as his ticket to freedom.

programme.title: Love Aaj Kal
programme.desc: Jai and Meera have had a good run as a couple, but work calls Meera to New Delhi, while Jai wants to move to San Francisco. Although the two love one another, their ambitions take precedence over their passion and amicably they decide to part ways. Veer Singh, a prosperous Sikh who runs the café at which Jai and Meera call it a day, finds the whole arrangement thoroughly unromantic and odd.

programme.title: Student of the Year
programme.desc: Dean Yoginder Vashisht, retired, has fallen gravely ill. Students from his final year at St Theresa's College rush to his side.

programme.title: Boss
programme.desc: Satyakant is an upright man of principles who banishes his son Surya after the boy makes some bad choices. Surya is taken under the wing of the transport king Big Boss.

programme.title: Department
programme.desc: A suspended cop joins an inspector's fight against organised crime.

programme.desc: The host interviews various media personalities, shares the latest news of the Bollywood industry and reviews the newest movies.

programme.title: Jaane Bhi Do Yaaro
programme.desc: Two photographers experience the deadly side of Bombay real estate when they stumble upon a murder.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: The MacDougals are invited to spend Easter with the Barones.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray gets upset when Debra shares intimate details about their relationship with her friends.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: A modelling agent tells Robert he has a unique look and convinces him to pose for head shots with their `in-house' photographer, but when Robert and Ray return for the pictures, they are surprised to find the modelling agency has mysteriously disappeared.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: A young man, Sam Gilula, arrives at Frank and Marie's and everyone is surprised when he and Frank fondly embrace. Sam explains Frank had been quite an inspiration to him as a lad, and Ray and Robert make fun of Frank, wondering how he could have inspired anyone.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Robert get into tricky territory over who will have responsibility for Marie in the future.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: `Strictly' star Anton Du Beke hands over his phone for Send to All, `Love Island' stars Dani Dyer and Samira Mighty help prank the latest Unexpected Star as the Theatre Royal is turned into a cinema, and Gino D'Acampo gets a rude awakening when he becomes the first celebrity to play The Midnight Gameshow. Also featuring music from pop superstar Cheryl.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts this skit comedy show where the actors on the show, usually Wayne Brady, Colin Mochrie, Ryan Stiles and another guest star or two do different comedy skits. It's all improv and made up on the spot.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Everybody Finds Out
programme.desc: Chandler and Monica's relationship becomes less of a secret, and Ross must prove to his boss that he's sane in order to return to work.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Girl Who Hits Joey
programme.desc: Chandler becomes anxious when he thinks Monica wants more from their relationship, and Ross moves into his new apartment and fails to impress his new neighbours. Meanwhile, Joey is afraid to end his relationship with a diminutive woman who punches him hard every time she thinks something is fun.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe finds a police officer's badge and goes on a spree of arrests until she meets up with the owner, while Joey wonders if he is in love with Monica.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachel's Inadvertent Kiss
programme.desc: Rachel has an interview for her dream job at Ralph Lauren. All goes well until she inadvertently kisses the interviewer, and at the second interview she mistakenly berates him for his `sexual intentions'. Meanwhile, Monica competes to have a better relationship than Phoebe's, and Joey is arrested for stalking a sexy neighbour with whom Ross gets a date.

programme.title: Criminally Funny
programme.desc: Featuring a tank on the run, juvenile offenders, and a cheerleader with anger issues.

programme.title: Criminally Funny
programme.desc: Featuring terrible truckers, feathered offenders, and a reptile on the run.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Following a rocky start in Red River Gorge, Barney's got another activity in store - a paranormal one, at the haunted Poasttown Elementary School. The next day, the Walshes wake up in Louisville, the home of Muhammad Ali. There they follow in the footsteps of `The Champ', sparring down at a local boxing ring to work up an appetite. Barney rewards his dad with a well-earned KFC.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: In Ohio, Brad and Barney head straight for Dayton, the home of aviation. After recreating history in the Wright B Flyer, it's Barney's turn to take the wheel - of a plane. The Walshes attend a local rodeo, and Brad's quick to realise they're not just there to spectate: they'll be taking part in `the most dangerous eight seconds in sport'. However, after a nasty fall from an angry bull, Brad ends up spending the night in hospital, giving `Breaking Dad' a whole new meaning.

programme.desc: A serial killer preys on women from a deprived area of Daytona Beach, Florida. Detectives have boxes of DNA and ballistic evidence, but they lack a suspect.

programme.desc: Jake hits on an attractive medical examiner but gets more than he bargained for. Meanwhile, Amy discovers Sgt. Jeffords has hidden artistic talents.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay and Manny set out to cut down their own Christmas tree. Meanwhile, Gloria is on edge when her mother visits. And Mitchell does last-minute shopping to find the perfect gift for Lily.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil ends up in jail after tricking Luke into taking a dance class. Meanwhile, Jay and Gloria plan birthday parties for Manny and Joe. Later, Mitch and Cam try to book a wedding venue.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Gloria settles a score with a 'mean girl' mum, and Mitch tries to out-green his neighbour.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: While Jay struggles to digest some news from best pal Shorty, Cam and Mitch take a break from the wedding planning to enjoy a romantic dinner.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Homer celebrate their 11th anniversary, but find that the sexual spark is gone. Meanwhile, Bart and Lisa go treasure hunting and find an alternative ending to a classic film.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer's evil hammock produces an army of Homer clones, zombie cowboys threaten to destroy Springfield after Lisa outlaws guns, and the Simpsons travel to an island inhabited by manimals.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: How I Spent My Strummer Vacation
programme.desc: Marge and the kids send Homer off to rock `n' roll camp to realise his dream of being a rock star.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Bart vs Lisa vs the 3rd Grade
programme.desc: Lisa has competition for the class brain role when she is promoted to the third grade. It comes from Bart, who's been demoted to the same class.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Charlie falls on his sword, but not everyone's ready to forgive and forget. Elsewhere, Misbah plays matchmaker, Frankie wants to do the right thing and Jenson supports Mercedes.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Marge gets a job at Homer's work, Mr Burns falls madly in love with her. He invites Marge over for an intimate dinner for two, complete with a kidnapped Tom Jones to serenade them.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.sub-title: Don't Mess with My Texas Wedding
programme.desc: Four more singles meet their matches at the altar. And it's not the first time some of them have said 'I do'.

programme.desc: A very hungover David Potts is grilled at the agency about his passionate date with Anthony. Lucinda Light's choices involve magic and vikings.

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: Naked Attraction

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Chartered surveyor and t'ai chi enthusiast John is looking for a woman who'll bring balance to his life; is he the yin to Sherry's yang? Hannah, who's 40, is looking for a man who shares her love of adventure.

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating

programme.desc: Four more singles meet their matches at the altar. And it's not the first time some of them have said 'I do'.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: At Pop-Pop's old apartment, Adam discovers an item from the past and realises they have more in common than he thought; Erica and Geoff search for a place to enjoy a moment alone.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly discovers the complicated world of adult relationships is filled with minefields when she opens her mind to dating; Barry discovers a new passion.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across the United States, travelling to New Jersey to try to snatch Italian restaurant Leone's from the jaws of failure. Owner Leone had a thriving business, but after a sudden illness she spent two years in hospital in a coma. When she returned, she was heartbroken to discover that her lazy son Michael had run all her efforts and the restaurant into the ground.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Harry wants a royal wedding and to be King for the day and plans to make his no-nonsense bride his Queen. But, bride-to-be Charlotte wants something low key.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: The Hutchinson family are faced with a devastating reality. Meanwhile, Charlie prepares to face the music, and Beth extends an olive branch to her estranged brother.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Charlie falls on his sword, but not everyone's ready to forgive and forget. Elsewhere, Misbah plays matchmaker, Frankie wants to do the right thing and Jenson supports Mercedes.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring sheep.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Jackie and Richard's evening features some celebrity name-dropping. There's more bragging than cooking for Ross and Hanne. And Gail and Nigel take a risk: will it pay off?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Orange County, Southern California, to try to save an Italian restaurant from imminent destruction. Luigi's was a successful restaurant with a great reputation until Dominic decided to retire to Italy and brought in son Tony to help run it. Familial fighting and an unappetising menu of 126 items have let the restaurant down the drain. Can Gordon fix it, or is Luigi's beyond repair?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In Atlanta, Gordon helps first-time restaurant owners Richard and Jorge. Their restaurant, Park's Edge, has been open for three years but is in big trouble due to constant fighting between them, poor food and a bad vibe in the tight-knit neighbourhood. Gordon comes up with a brilliant plan to resolve the issue within the community, but then a rogue chef threatens to ruin everything.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.sub-title: Don't Mess with My Texas Wedding
programme.desc: Four more singles meet their matches at the altar. And it's not the first time some of them have said 'I do'.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil gets a new RV for a fun family road trip, Jay and Gloria search for Manny's backpack and Lily's gymnastics meet brings out Cam and Mitch's competitive spirit.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Cam and Mitch have a memorable summer.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Krusty shuns his old routines in favour of edgier material and suddenly becomes the hottest comic in show business.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer is arrested for an insurance scam that Moe hatched to impress his new girlfriend.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge poses for a calendar for charity, but she is soon the gossip of the city after she shows a little too much during her photo shoot.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa accepts an invitation to join a coven of wiccans and soon finds herself the chief witness in their trial for witchcraft. Meanwhile, Homer starts to hang out with his new pal Cletus after discovering the slack-jawed yokel makes moonshine.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake is frustrated when a detective from Major Crimes takes over a murder case that he is close to solving and steals his thunder. Determined to outwit `The Vulture', he enlists his colleagues to help him find the murder weapon and close the case.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake bets Captain Holt that he can steal his Medal of Valor before midnight and Amy must overcome her hatred of Halloween.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay misinterprets Gloria's anger over a dream she thinks he had for another offence he committed.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Luke competes against the son of Phil's nemesis in a wrestling match.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: While Cam's thunder is stolen by a popular teacher's return, Mitch doesn't know what to make of his former college pal-turned-new boss.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: A leisurely day at the museum proves to be anything but for Cam, Mitch and Manny. Plus, Phil helps Andy with his anniversary gift.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge gets breast implants meant for a younger patient, and Bart and Milhouse spot Krusty on an episode of `Batman and Robin'.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons' house has to be fumigated after an ice hockey player gives his termite-infested stick to Lisa, and the only suitable place the family can find to live is in a TV reality show about life in the 1890s.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Someone tries to murder Homer in the steam room at the Stagnant Springs Spa. Needing help from someone who understands the mind of a killer, Chief Wiggum invites Sideshow Bob out of prison to help investigate the case, but has Bob got over his compulsion to kill Bart, and can he work out who Homer could possibly have annoyed enough to provide a motive for murder?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart feels sorry for Mrs Krabapple after Skinner stands her up again to attend to his mother's whims, so Lisa suggests he should nominate her for Teacher of the Year. However, Skinner worries that he could lose her if she wins.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Prince is smitten with Vicky, but does she feel the same? Warren makes a chilling discovery, and Nancy reaches breaking point.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer gets kicked out of an all-you-can-eat seafood restaurant for eating too much, he takes the owner to court. The restaurant offers a peace deal: Homer can eat all the food he likes so long as people can gawk at him like a freak in return. Meanwhile, Bart falls in love with his babysitter.

programme.desc: Wedding bells ring for the final pair in this year's experiment, before all five couples jet off to Panama for their honeymoons.

programme.title: The Black Swan
programme.desc: With the pardon of notorious pirate Henry Morgan by the English King, Caribbean pirates are offered amnesty if they give up their lawless ways. As governor of Jamaica, Morgan picks Captain Jamie Waring to round up the renegade captain Billy Leech. Beautiful Margaret Denby complicates matters for Waring, as her scorn for him is matched only by his love for her.

programme.title: Yellow Sky
programme.desc: Seven outlaws rob a bank in Arizona then head for the badlands where they encounter an old man and his granddaughter, who have a hidden cache of gold. The outlaws make a deal to share it but some of them choose to cheat. Their leader, who has fallen for the granddaughter, takes on the double-crossers, finally facing one of them in a show-down.

programme.title: Lifeboat
programme.desc: Tensions erupt between the survivors of a sunken ocean liner and the Nazi responsible for the disaster.

programme.title: Heaven Knows, Mr Allison
programme.desc: A Roman Catholic nun and a hard-bitten US Marine are stranded together on a Japanese-occupied island in the South Pacific during World War II. Under constant threat of discovery by a ruthless enemy, they hide in a cave and forage for food together. Their forced companionship and the struggle for survival forge a powerful emotional bond between them.

programme.title: Gemini Man
programme.desc: Henry Brogan is an elite assassin who becomes the target of a mysterious operative who can seemingly predict his every move. To his horror, he soon learns that the man who's trying to kill him is a younger, faster, cloned version of himself.

programme.title: Renfield
programme.desc: Renfield, the tortured aide to his narcissistic boss, Dracula, is forced to procure his master's prey and do his every bidding. However, after centuries of servitude, he's ready to see if there's a life outside the shadow of the Prince of Darkness.

programme.title: How to Have Sex
programme.desc: Three British teenage girls go on a rites-of-passage holiday - drinking, clubbing and hooking up, in what should be the best summer of their lives.

programme.title: The Lobster
programme.desc: Single people must find romantic partners in 45 days or be transformed into beasts.
