programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Glassblower Belinda from Northamptonshire and upcyclers Becky and Paul from Oxfordshire are hoping Dom and his team can help them turn their passions into profits. Belinda has only been glass-blowing for three years and has recently graduated from university. Becky and husband Paul met and married after their partners both passed away. Now they want to establish a business that will support the two of them so that Paul can give up his painting and decorating job, and they can work together.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Mike and Sarah are looking to change their lives in the marquee. Labourer Mike has a passion for turning wooden bowls, but is held back by his struggle for perfection. Mentor Alice hopes helping him see the beauty in the imperfections in his work will be the key to unlocking a profitable business. Sarah's life changed during the pandemic when she quit her job and took up pottery. One year on and she needs to start making money from her craft.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team have a record number of cars to auction, but Derek finds room for one more - a replica AC Cobra. A couple are reunited with their Volvo breakdown truck.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A classic car fan with a love of the Jaguar E-Type reluctantly sends his dream car to auction, hoping to buy a motorhome. Two Ford Escorts get Dave's pulse racing.

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: This episode tells the story of the biggest single killing year in the history of the Final Solution and how Jews, who eventually died at Auschwitz, came from places as far away as the Channel Islands.

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.sub-title: The First Six Months in Power
programme.desc: Within the first few months in power, Göring eradicated the Nazis' parliamentary opposition. These months also saw the rise of Henrich Himmler, the unassuming, uncharismatic head of an elite force of fanatical Nazis known as the SS.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team look back to choose their favourite moments of series 31 and update some of the star items valued. Includes the moment when a £1 bootsale buy went on to become the best investment in Roadshow history, with footage from the exciting auction.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: At Samares Manor in Jersey the experts scrutinise objects from Japan, South Africa, Egypt, America and France. One of the most valuable watches ever seen on the show is uncovered, and there is the bizarre find of Marilyn Monroe's lemon squeezer. In a first for the 32-year-old series, a forensic test is performed live to prove the worth of a gold bangle.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek rubs shoulders with pop music royalty when he picks up a classic Morgan sports car. A World War Two ambulance arrives at Thornton le Dale and a Ford Model T needs a new home.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: This week at the Mathewsons', there is a 1982 Audi Quattro and a Sunbeam Alpine. Plus, Derek takes delivery of a beautiful green Renault van, previously used as a bread van in Paris.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Life on-board a narrowboat with Robbie Cumming. Robbie tackles a propeller problem and tricky locks on the Trent and Mersey Canal in Derbyshire.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: A low canal tunnel in Staffordshire proves a problem and Robbie helps out a stranded boater.

programme.desc: Derek checks out an abandoned 1985 Renault 4 and an MGB Roadster that hasn't seen the light of day for 36 years, and Jack heads to Rotherham to pick up a pair of rare Bond Bugs.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Ceramicist Izzy from London and stone carver Luke from the Scottish Borders step into the marquee hoping to turn their passions and skills into successful businesses. It's high stakes for Izzy as she's already given up her job in advertising to try and make it as a full time potter. For Luke, dyslexia, and years of being bullied at school have knocked his confidence, preventing him from putting himself and his pieces out there, but Dom and mentor Louis can see just how talented he is.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Dom welcomes willow weaver Dea and artist Gemma into the marquee. With chronic fatigue syndrome along with autism and ADHD, Dea dreams of being able to build a home business that suits her life. After being told art doesn't pay by a careers advisor at school, Gemma gave up on her artistic dreams and became a teacher. She continued to create art as a hobby and after some recent success selling her art at a market she's hoping mentor Sharon can help her finally become a full-time artist.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A widow reluctantly parts with her husband's treasured collection of Rolls Royces and Bentleys, and an impulse buyer finds one of the cars a new home.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A near-perfect 50s Chevy panel van and a very desirable low-mileage 80s Brooklands Capri tempt auctioneer Derek out of Yorkshire.

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: By 1943, life was good for many of the SS. Eyewitness accounts recall how those in power at Auschwitz lined their pockets with wealth stolen from Jewish inmates while also engaging in illicit affairs.

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Adolf Hitler had been chancellor of Germany for just under a year and it was a challenging balancing act. On the one hand the Nazis had to be be mindful of president Paul von Hindenburg and the vice chancellor Franz von Papen, who were members of Germany's traditional aristocratic and ruling elite.Then, there was the Nazis' own power base, the stormtroopers, made up of millions of angry, disenfranchised men who wreaked havoc on the streets of Germany.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce takes the team to the Lancashire coast for a busy day in the magnificent Blackpool Tower Ballroom. Objects under discussion include a bible containing handwritten extracts from Charlotte Bronte; powerful images drawn under fire by a war artist; and a boot sale brooch that makes the owner want to scream with delight when she hears the valuation.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return for a second visit to the Tower Ballroom in Blackpool. Amongst the objects under the experts' eye are one of the earliest prayer books seen on the programme and a valuable pair of duelling pistols, while the original Teddy from Watch with Mother makes a surprise appearance.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Scotland in the mother of all rainstorms to pick up a Jaguar E-type and a rare MGC convertible. Plus, Dave extracts a rare Austin Gipsey from a lock up.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: There's a surprise waiting for Derek in Hull - a MGSA sports car that once had a tree growing through it. Plus, in North Wales, Alan Holmes is selling his son's Ford Escort RS 1600i.

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: By 1943, life was good for many of the SS. Eyewitness accounts recall how those in power at Auschwitz lined their pockets with wealth stolen from Jewish inmates while also engaging in illicit affairs.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: A fallen tree and a leaking lock pound hamper Robbie's journey across the Pennines.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie runs aground on the Rochdale Canal, will he make it to Manchester?

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A CBD80 battery-powered loco needs to be hauled 20 miles from a factory in Burton-on-Trent to the heritage Chasewater Railway in Staffordshire.

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: 300,000 sq miles of ocean. Three GPS-tracked voyages, filmed around the clock. One week at sea. Emilia Jayne hunts for cuttlefish in the world's busiest shipping lane. Cargo ships have right of way here, but lucrative cuttlefish swim in these grounds too. After finding good fishing at the start of his week-long trip, can skipper Luke Selvey keep the secret to himself? Margaret of Ladram is Brixham's most successful trawler.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Scotland in the mother of all rainstorms to pick up a Jaguar E-type and a rare MGC convertible. Plus, Dave extracts a rare Austin Gipsey from a lock up.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: There's a surprise waiting for Derek in Hull - a MGSA sports car that once had a tree growing through it. Plus, in North Wales, Alan Holmes is selling his son's Ford Escort RS 1600i.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus and Will find an Audi Quattro roadster that has seen better days. It is full of problems and has not seen a service in years. Clearly, they need to buy it.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien find, fix and flog unwanted possessions. At the fairground, the boys pick up a bicycle made for two, a ship's search lamp and a lambing chair.

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James goes off-road to tell the story of how the 4x4 conquered the world. From the wartime jeep and the global struggle between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, to the white-knuckle world of rallying and boy racers. James conquers Mount Fuji and races through the sun scorched Mojave Desert to decide which is the ultimate people's 4x4.
